response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with broad web, mobile, and some systems experience, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position hard-requires native-level Japanese proficiency, deep C/UNIX development, Linux kernel source modification experience, and embedded LAN-switch security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 networking) — none of which appear on the resume. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a 7-month stint in 2014-2015, and his career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js product work.
strengths: Long, consistent engineering career with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Some low-level and systems experience (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux)
Demonstrated ownership and initiative through open-source projects and technical workshops
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a strict native-level requirement for this role
No embedded networking or security-domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, switch/L2-L3, kernel modification)
C programming experience is dated and brief; recent decade is predominantly web/full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript

key_factors.factor: General engineering seniority and systems fundamentals
